experience: Attending the pre-dawn wholesale market at Huangsha Aquatic Products Market, where restaurants negotiate live seafood purchases, and eating congee with workers at 4am in the surrounding stalls
where: Huangsha Aquatic Products Market, Liwan District, Guangzhou
why_distinctive: The working-meal culture around wholesale markets — congee shops serving dock workers before dawn — is a documented layer of Cantonese food life distinct from any restaurant experience. The negotiation rituals between buyers and fishmongers follow conventions invisible to leisure tourists.
source: Documented in Fuchsia Dunlop's 'Shark's Fin and Sichuan Pepper' (2008, Norton) and corroborated in multiple threads on r/Chinavisa and r/movingtochina; also referenced in Lucky Peach issue #1 (2011) in the context of Cantonese market culture. NOTE: I cannot verify exact page numbers — treat as a direction for your own verification.
